AI-Powered Workflows

Automated prospecting pipelines that find leads, score them, enrich with contact data, and deliver ready-to-close lists.

Workflows

Per Sweep
🗺️ Local Map Sweeper Google Maps → Scored Leads
Finds local businesses with weak marketing, scores them Hot/Warm/Low, enriches with emails, lets you review and remove, then compiles a ready-to-outreach lead list.
Step 1
Search Google Maps via Apify
Searches Google Maps for your target niche and city. Pulls every matching business with name, address, phone, website, reviews, and rating. Falls back to Google Places API if needed.
ApifyGoogle Places API
Step 2
Enrich with Website & Owner Data
Checks each business website for owner names, copyright dates, and site quality signals. Identifies decision-makers from About pages, team pages, and LinkedIn.
Web Scraper
Step 3
Score Leads: Hot / Warm / Low
🔥 Hot: Under 50 reviews, below 4.0 stars, no website — maximum opportunity.
🟡 Warm: 50-150 reviews, has website but outdated, 4.0-4.5 stars.
⚪ Low: Over 150 reviews, 4.5+ stars, active website — already established.
🔥 Hot🟡 Warm⚪ Low
Step 4
Review & Remove Leads
Pauses and presents the scored list. You pick which leads to keep and which to drop before any emails are sent or contacts are enriched.
👤 Operator Review
Step 5
Enrich with Email via Hunter.io
Looks up owner and decision-maker emails for every remaining lead using Hunter.io. Shows confidence scores so you know which emails will land.
Hunter.io
Step 6
Write Custom Outreach Hooks
Writes a personalized 1-sentence outreach hook for each lead. References something specific: their missing website, outdated copyright, review gap, or SEO weakness. No generic hooks.
Step 7
Compile & Save Lead List
Exports the final ranked list as CSV and markdown. Includes: rank, score tier, business name, address, phone, email, website, reviews, rating, decision-maker name, and outreach hook.
📄 CSV📋 Markdown
💼 LinkedIn Sales Nav Extractor Sales Navigator → ICP-Scored Leads
Extracts B2B decision-makers from a LinkedIn Sales Navigator search, enriches with email and phone, scores against your ICP criteria, and produces a ranked outreach list.
Step 1
Extract Profiles from Sales Navigator
Walks through your Sales Navigator search results page by page. Captures full name, job title, company, size, industry, location, LinkedIn URL, and recent activity signals.
LinkedIn Sales Navigator
Step 2
Enrich Contact Data
Finds verified work email via Hunter.io, Apollo, or Clearbit. Optionally finds phone numbers. Captures tenure in current role and last LinkedIn activity date.
Hunter.ioApolloClearbit
Step 3
Apply Filters
Configurable filters: require verified email, require phone, require recent LinkedIn activity (posted in last 30 days). Drops leads that don't meet your criteria.
Step 4
Score Against ICP (100-Point Rubric)
35 pts: Title fit (exact match to your ideal titles).
25 pts: Company size fit (matches your target band).
20 pts: Tenure sweet spot (1-4 years in role).
20 pts: Activity signal (posted on LinkedIn in last 30 days).
Step 5
Rank & Trim
Sorts by score descending. Drops anyone below 50 points. Keeps your top N leads — only the ones worth your time.
Step 6
Compile Lead List
Exports the final list with: rank, name, title, company, size, industry, location, verified email, phone, LinkedIn URL, last activity, and ICP score.
📄 CSV📋 Markdown
📘 How to Use: LinkedIn Sales Nav Extractor Setup → Tune → Scale
Three steps from setup to first run, plus tuning tips after your first 3 batches.
Setup Step 1
Build Your Sales Nav Search
In LinkedIn Sales Navigator, build a search using all the filters that matter: industry, role, region, company size. Save the search URL. This URL is what you hand to the extractor.
LinkedIn Sales Navigator
Setup Step 2
Configure Enrichment
Pick your email provider (Hunter is the default). Decide if you need phone numbers. Choose your filters: require verified email, require phone, require recent LinkedIn activity.
Hunter.ioApolloClearbit
Setup Step 3
Review the First Batch by Hand
Spot-check the first 10-15 leads. Confirm titles, companies, and emails are accurate. If they're off, narrow your Sales Nav search and re-run. This calibration step saves you from pushing bad data downstream.
👤 Manual QA
🔧 Tuning Tips (After Your First 3 Runs)
Email match rate is low
Hunter and similar providers vary by industry. If your match rate is under 60%, try Apollo or Clearbit. Some industries (small private companies) are just hard to enrich.
Wrong-fit titles slipping through
Sales Nav title filtering is fuzzy. Tighten your search by adding boolean exclusions, e.g., NOT "manager" if you only want directors and above.
Volume too low
If you're extracting under 20 leads per run, your Sales Nav search is too tight. Widen company size, region, or seniority. Volume up first, then quality up via scoring.
⚠️ Don't skip the validator
This workflow hands its raw output to the Lead Validator. Don't skip that step. Pushing unverified leads into your CRM costs you sender reputation, wasted sales time, and embarrassing follow-ups to dead businesses.
💰 Funding Announcement Tracker Daily Scan → Newly Funded Companies
Daily scan of TechCrunch, Crunchbase, PR Newswire, and VentureBeat for companies that just raised money in your target verticals. Companies with fresh capital are buying — this gets you there first.
Step 1
Scan Funding Announcements
Pulls all funding announcements from configured sources over the lookback window. Captures company name, round size, stage, investors, description, founding team, announcement date, and source URL.
TechCrunchCrunchbasePR NewswireVentureBeat
Step 2
Filter by ICP Criteria
Drops companies outside your target verticals, funding stages, round size range, and regions. Only companies that match your buyer profile make it through.
Step 3
Enrich Company & Decision-Maker
Finds company website, employee count, HQ city, and the decision-maker matching your target titles (e.g. Head of Marketing, VP Sales). Gets their email and LinkedIn URL.
Hunter.ioLinkedIn
Step 4
Write Funding-Specific Outreach Hooks
Writes a 2-sentence opener referencing the specific funding event: round size, stage, and what they'll likely spend on next. Timely and specific, not generic congrats.
Step 5
Compile & Chain to Validator
Exports the list with company, vertical, stage, round size, date, source URL, HQ, employees, decision-maker, email, LinkedIn, and outreach hook. Chains to Lead Validator.
📄 CSV🔗 → Lead Validator
📋 Job Posting Buyer Intent Daily Scan → Hiring = Buying Signal
Companies hiring for a specific role are signaling they need a specific solution. Hiring a VP Sales? They need sales tooling. Hiring a social media manager? They need content help. This workflow scans job boards daily and builds a target list of companies with active buying intent.
Step 1
Scan Job Boards for Trigger Roles
Searches Indeed and LinkedIn Jobs for postings matching your trigger titles (e.g. "VP Sales", "Head of Marketing"). Captures company name, role, posting date, location, and posting URL.
IndeedLinkedIn Jobs
Step 2
Filter by ICP Criteria
Drops companies outside your target industries, size bands, and regions. Deduplicates the same company posting across multiple boards.
Step 3
Infer Buying Need from Role
Maps each trigger role to the need it signals: hiring VP Sales → needs pipeline infrastructure. Hiring Head of Marketing → needs lead gen and content. Connects the inferred need to your offer.
Step 4
Enrich Buyer Contact Data
Finds the actual buyer (one level above the role being hired). If they're hiring a VP Sales, the buyer is the CEO or COO. Gets their name, title, email, and LinkedIn.
Hunter.ioLinkedIn
Step 5
Write Outreach Hooks
Writes a 2-sentence hook referencing the open role and inferred need. Helpful, not creepy: "Saw you're hiring a VP Sales. While you're searching, we help companies at your stage build the pipeline so the new VP hits the ground running."
Step 6
Compile & Chain to Validator
Exports the list with company, role, posting date, inferred need, buyer name, title, email, LinkedIn, outreach hook, and posting URL. Chains to Lead Validator.
📄 CSV🔗 → Lead Validator
🔧 Tuning Tips
Pick the right trigger roles. Start with 3-5 specific titles, not 20. The narrower the trigger, the cleaner the inferred need.
Map roles to your offer. Be honest: which roles would actually buy what you sell? If your offer doesn't map cleanly to a hiring trigger, this workflow won't help.
Reach out within 14 days. After 30 days the role is filled or the urgency has faded. The fresh window is when this works best.
🕵️ Competitor Customer Mapper Top of Funnel → Steal Their Customers
Give it a competitor's name. It scans their case studies, testimonial pages, and customer logos, then builds a list of companies already buying that category — so you can pitch your alternative.
Step 1
Scrape Competitor Customer Pages
Visits each competitor's website and inspects /customers, /case-studies, /testimonials, /reviews, /pricing, and /blog pages. Extracts customer logos, named case study companies, testimonial sources, and any company mentioned in reviews or quotes.
Web Scraper
Step 2
Dedupe & Verify Companies
Combines all extracted companies, removes duplicates, and verifies each one is a real, currently operating business. Tags companies that appear as customers of multiple competitors.
Step 3
Score Fit Against Your ICP
40 pts: Industry match. 30 pts: Company size match. 20 pts: Geographic match. 10 pts: Buyer freshness (case study published in last 12 months = still actively a customer).
Step 4
Enrich Decision-Makers
For each qualifying company, finds the decision-maker: name, title, work email, and LinkedIn URL. Targets the role most relevant to your offer.
Hunter.ioLinkedIn
Step 5
Write Outreach Angles
Writes a 2-sentence outreach angle per lead that references the specific competitor they use. Framed as curious, not adversarial: "We work with teams using [Competitor] who…"
Step 6
Compile & Chain to Validator
Exports the ranked list with company, industry, size, region, competitor used, decision-maker, email, LinkedIn, fit score, and outreach angle. Chains to the Lead Validator before CRM push.
📄 CSV🔗 → Lead Validator
🔧 Tuning Tips
List too small? Add 1-2 more competitors, or check their G2 reviews and partner pages instead of just /customers.
Outreach feels pushy? Reframe as "we work with teams using [competitor] who…" — curious, not adversarial.
Wrong decision-makers? Specify exact target titles in your ICP. Getting CFOs when you sell to CMOs means the title matching is too broad.
Run quarterly. Competitors publish new case studies regularly. Quarterly runs keep your target list fresh.
🎙️ Podcast Guest Finder Authority Building → Get Booked on Shows
Paste a podcast topic or niche. The workflow finds podcasts in that space, identifies the host, surfaces their booking process and contact details, scores fit, and writes a personalized 3-sentence pitch referencing a specific recent episode.
Step 1
Search Podcast Directories
Searches Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Listen Notes, and Podchaser for podcasts matching your niche. Captures podcast name, host, release cadence, episode count, recent episodes, audience size, and booking URL.
AppleSpotifyListen NotesPodchaser
Step 2
Filter for Active & Relevant
Drops dormant podcasts (fewer than 3 episodes in 90 days), podcasts below your audience minimum, wrong region/language, and false-positive topic matches.
Step 3
Score Podcast Fit
35 pts: Topic alignment with your angle. 30 pts: Audience matches your ICP. 20 pts: Booking ease (clear guest page or track record). 15 pts: Host accessibility (public email, LinkedIn, Twitter).
Step 4
Enrich Host Contact Data
Finds host's full name, email, LinkedIn URL, Twitter/X handle, and a recent activity signal (latest post or episode topic).
Hunter.ioLinkedInTwitter/X
Step 5
Write Personalized 3-Sentence Pitches
Each pitch: (1) references a specific recent episode by name, (2) connects your angle to that episode's topic, (3) proposes a concrete interview topic. Feels like it was written by someone who actually listened.
Step 6
Compile & Chain to Validator
Exports the list with podcast name, host, email, LinkedIn, Twitter, audience, booking URL, fit score, referenced episode, and pitch. Chains to Lead Validator.
📄 CSV🔗 → Lead Validator
🔧 Tuning Tips
Tighten your niche. "Marketing podcasts" is too broad. "Podcasts for B2B SaaS marketers about demand gen" is right.
Be honest about your angle. A good angle = topic + counter-intuitive POV + proof. "I'm an expert in AI" is templated. "I built and sold a 7-figure agency using AI workflows in 2024" is specific.
Send pitches yourself. The workflow gets you 90% there. The last 10% (final personalization, sending from your real account, follow-up) needs to be human.
👁️ LinkedIn Activity Watcher Real-Time → Buying Moment Alerts
Monitors 30-50 high-value LinkedIn profiles for posts, comments, and job changes. When a target posts about a pain point you solve, you get an alert with a suggested opener within hours — not days.
Step 1
Poll Target Profiles
Visits each monitored profile and checks for new posts, comments on others' posts, job changes (new title or company), and public mentions. Captures full text and URL of each activity.
LinkedIn
Step 2
Evaluate Trigger Relevance
Scores each activity against your trigger keywords (e.g. "hiring", "frustrated", "scaling", "need help"). Tags activity type and scores relevance 0-100 against your offer description.
Step 3
Fire Real-Time Alerts
Sends an alert if: keywords match, job change detected, or relevance score exceeds 70. Each alert includes what they did, why it matters, and a suggested 1-sentence opener for your reply.
📱 Telegram📧 Email💬 Slack
Step 4
Log Activity & Compile Digest
Saves every detected activity to a running log. Compiles a daily digest of all activities sorted by relevance so you can spot patterns over time.
📊 Daily Digest📋 Activity Log
🔧 Tuning Tips
Too many alerts? Tighten keywords or raise relevance threshold from 70 to 80. Alert volume should match the time you have to respond.
Alerts feel irrelevant? "Marketing" fires constantly and means nothing. "Looking for a marketing agency" fires rarely and means everything.
Watch 30-50 profiles, not 500. More profiles = more noise. Only watch targets where timing genuinely matters.
Act within 4 hours. The whole point is speed. If you can't respond same-day, use the daily digest instead of real-time alerts.
🎯 Buyer Persona Scorer Qualification → A/B/C/D Tier Filter
Define your ideal customer once: revenue range, team size, industry, region, buyer titles. Every new lead gets auto-scored against that profile. Your default "is this even worth my time?" filter — chains after any prospecting workflow.
Step 1
Score Industry Match (25 pts)
Full points if the lead's industry is in your target list. 10 points for adjacent industries. Zero for everything else.
Step 2
Score Revenue Fit (25 pts)
Full points if revenue falls within your range. Loses 1 point per 10% deviation outside the band. Below range = can't afford you. Above range = different buying process.
Step 3
Score Team Size Fit (20 pts)
Full points if team size is in your sweet spot. Below minimum = too small to afford. Above maximum = too bureaucratic to close.
Step 4
Score Region & Title Match (25 pts)
10 points for region match. 15 points if a contact has one of your target buyer titles (8 for adjacent titles like "Director" when you target "VP").
Step 5
Apply Disqualifiers & Compute Final Score
Hard-no criteria get -50 points each (PE-owned, gov contracts, etc.). Final score tags each lead: A-tier (80+), B-tier (60-79), C-tier (40-59), D-tier (<40). Includes gap reasons explaining every deduction.
🔧 Tuning Tips
Everyone is A-tier? Your ICP is too lenient. Tighten revenue or team-size bands. A real ICP rejects most leads.
No one is A-tier? ICP is too tight, or your prospecting workflow is producing leads in the wrong ballpark.
Disqualifiers misfiring? Disqualifiers should be hard nos, not soft penalties. If it's negotiable, reduce its weight or remove it.
Refine after 30 days. Track which tier actually closed deals. Adjust your ICP based on real data, not gut feel.
✅ The Lead Validator Chained Step → Every Workflow Ends Here
Every prospecting workflow feeds into this. Takes raw leads, verifies each one is real, checks email deliverability, dedupes against your CRM, and pushes only clean, verified leads to your destination tool. Don't skip it.
Step 1
Business Operating Check
Visits each business website to confirm it loads with current content (not parked, not 404, not "domain for sale"). For businesses without websites, searches directories to confirm they still exist.
Web Verification
Step 2
Email Deliverability Check
Validates every email: correct format, working MX records, mailbox accepts mail. Tags each as deliverable, risky, or dead. Risky emails are treated as dead unless you override.
Hunter.ioMX Lookup
Step 3
Phone Number Format Check
Confirms correct country code, digit count, and checks against known spam or carrier-default numbers. Format validation only — no live calls.
Step 4
Duplicate Check
Compares each lead against your existing CRM contacts under the same tag. Matches on business name + city or matching domain. Duplicates are skipped automatically.
CRM Dedup
Step 5
Compute Final Score & Split
Adjusts the original workflow score: -30 if business not operating, -25 if primary email dead, -10 if phone invalid. Splits into two lists: CLEAN (score ≥ 60) and DROPPED (with reasons).
Step 6
Push Clean Leads to CRM
Pushes every verified lead to your destination tool with the tag you specified. Saves the dropped list as CSV for manual review. Sends you a summary: "Validated N leads. X passed. Y dropped."
📤 CRM Push📄 CSV (Dropped)
⚠️ Why This Step Exists
Sending unverified leads into your outreach kills your sender reputation, wastes your sales team's time, and leads to embarrassing follow-ups to dead businesses. Every prospecting workflow in this library chains into the Lead Validator before anything touches your CRM.
